name: First Cash Pawn
description_short: First Cash Pawn at 1912 W Pecan St #105A, Pflugerville, TX 78660 in the Watson Park neighborhood is Mon-Fri 9:00 AM – 7:00 PM. Google reviewers rate the branch 4.1 stars across 78 reviews. Call +1 512-989-2105.
description_long:
FirstCash, Inc. is headquartered in Fort Worth, Texas and operates as the largest international pawn shop chain with more than 3,300 retail locations. The company was founded to serve cash and credit-constrained consumers who need quick access to funds or affordable merchandise. FirstCash operates pawn stores across 29 U.S. states plus Washington D.C., the United Kingdom, and throughout Latin America including Mexico, Guatemala, Colombia, and El Salvador, employing approximately 22,000 people globally.

FirstCash's primary service is making small non-recourse pawn loans secured by pledged personal property, meaning customers receive cash immediately in exchange for leaving items as collateral. The company also operates a substantial retail business buying and selling a wide variety of merchandise including jewelry, electronics, tools, appliances, sporting goods, and musical instruments. Additional services include gold and precious metal buying, layaway plans with 10% down payments, and through its subsidiary AFF, lease-to-own and retail finance payment solutions through 15,000+ merchant partner locations nationwide.

FirstCash distinguishes itself through its massive scale as the leading international pawn operator and its dual business model combining pawn lending with a full retail operation. The company is publicly traded and included in both the S&P MidCap 400 Index and Russell 2000 Index, indicating institutional investor confidence. Their store locator tool and online inventory system provide transparency about available merchandise and locations, and they maintain active gold buying programs that appeal to customers needing immediate cash for precious metals.

While FirstCash provides essential short-term liquidity for credit-constrained consumers, pawn loans involve surrendering personal property and the risk of loss if items aren't reclaimed. The interest rates and terms of pawn loans are not disclosed on their website, making it difficult to compare costs with other lending alternatives. Customers should understand that pawn loans, while faster than traditional lending, represent a secured borrowing mechanism where failure to repay results in permanent loss of the pledged item.
pros: Massive network of 3,300+ locations across 29 U.S. states, DC, UK, and Latin America for convenient access
Immediate cash on the spot for pawn loans secured by personal property
No credit checks required for pawn loans, serving credit-constrained consumers
Diverse merchandise buying including jewelry, gold, electronics, tools, instruments, and appliances
Additional layaway service option with affordable 10% down payments for budget-conscious shoppers
Publicly traded company (S&P MidCap 400 and Russell 2000 indexed) providing institutional credibility
Retail sales component offers access to affordable merchandise alongside lending services
cons: No interest rates or loan terms disclosed on website, making cost comparison impossible
Pawn loans require surrendering personal property with risk of permanent loss if not repaid
Limited transparency about typical loan amounts, repayment periods, and renewal options
Non-recourse nature means items are forfeited without further collection efforts, incentivizing high interest rates
best_for: Cash-constrained consumers who need immediate funds and own valuable items to pledge
Shoppers seeking affordable merchandise through layaway plans or retail inventory
People with poor credit who cannot qualify for traditional personal loans
Individuals looking to sell gold, jewelry, or other valuables for quick cash
diagnosis: FirstCash is best for cash-constrained or credit-challenged consumers who own valuable items and need immediate funds, or for budget-conscious shoppers seeking affordable merchandise through layaway. The critical caveat is that pawn loans involve surrendering personal property with no recourse if unable to repay, and the lack of disclosed interest rates and terms makes it impossible to evaluate whether costs are competitive with alternative lending options.
services: Pawn loans secured by personal property (non-recourse)
Retail sales of jewelry, electronics, tools, appliances, sporting goods, and musical instruments
Gold and precious metal buying with cash on spot
Layaway service with 10% down payment option
Lease-to-own financing through subsidiary AFF at 15,000+ merchant partners
Buy and sell services for merchandise
